criterion: `createEntityStore` exposes the requested generic API, stays React Query-free, and provides one Zustand store per factory instance with per-entity state keyed by `entityId`.
evidence: Verified by Claude-as-reviewer in review.json (verdict=approved, 0 rework items). User signs off based on Claude review approval.

criterion: The store implements atomic bootstrap, lazy per-entity load, optimistic updates, text-field flush, revert, reset, save, saveImmediate, and error handling without discarding optimistic local state.
evidence: Verified by Claude-as-reviewer in review.json (verdict=approved, 0 rework items). User signs off based on Claude review approval.

criterion: The store uses an explicit clean-baseline model so `reset()` leaves an entity clean while `hasPersistedData` remains separately tracked.
evidence: Verified by Claude-as-reviewer in review.json (verdict=approved, 0 rework items). User signs off based on Claude review approval.

criterion: The store exposes only minimal internal dirty/pending inspection and external-sync helpers needed for adapter lifecycle flushing and safe query reconciliation, without widening the public `EntityView<T>` contract beyond M1 needs.
evidence: Verified by Claude-as-reviewer in review.json (verdict=approved, 0 rework items). User signs off based on Claude review approval.

criterion: `useEntity(entityId)` subscriptions are isolated so updates to one entity do not re-render another entity consumer.
evidence: Verified by Claude-as-reviewer in review.json (verdict=approved, 0 rework items). User signs off based on Claude review approval.

criterion: `useJoinClipsSettings(projectId)` is ported to the store factory while preserving the full existing public hook contract used by current callers.

criterion: Join Clips keeps `useToolSettings` as the only authoritative merged-load and readiness source and does not introduce a second merged-settings read path in M1.
evidence: Verified by Claude-as-reviewer in review.json (verdict=approved, 0 rework items). User signs off based on Claude review approval.
